ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Dec-18-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Caroline Hinshaw appeared by zoom for and with Dongna Fang (Trustee) by zoom.
David Huang also appeared by zoom.

Ms. Hinshaw responded to the Court's questions stating that she believes there are still fees that were previously approved that are unpaid, that there is a cash flow issue, that she needs to speak with Ms. Fang, that she is unfamiliar with the current status and that Ms. Fang is having issues with the tenants. Ms. Hinshaw also provided history as to how they created the trust and stated that Ms. Fang is trying to figure out what to do. Ms. Fang stated that she is thinking of selling the East Bay property, that emergency repairs to the property are going on right now, and that there are lots of repair issues and that is why she is thinking of selling the property. The Court stated that they need to consider supply and demand, how much money is going in, and how much is going out. Ms. Fang stated that she is thinking to sell and invest to get some cash flow. Ms. Hinshaw stated that Ms. Fang has been thinking about selling for some time, that Ms. Fang is a very diligent person and is diligent about in taking care of the Beneficiary. The Court informed the Parties that it is considering not having the Beneficiary cover the costs of the overdraft. With respect to the request to modify the 01/25/2019 order, the Court stated that Local Rule 14.78 is not relevant and that it does not see any evidence that the two-bid process is onerous. Ms. Hinshaw stated that if the workers are not taking the job, they don't want to take the time to provide bids. The Court stated that $26,000.00 in cash withdrawals is a lot, questions whether it should allow an allowance instead of being open ended and stated that it is not governed. Ms. Hinshaw stated that a lot of it is connected with travel, that the goal is to make the Beneficiary's life as comfortable as possible and that she will keep it in mind for the next accounting. The Court also noted that if funds are moved from a parcel to investments, they will need to think about an Investment Advisor and that it will reduce the trustee's fees. Ms. Hinshaw and Ms. Fang stated that they understand. The court asked Mr. Huang how he is doing, if he has anything he wants to say to the Court and if he has any concerns. Mr. Huang stated he is doing well today, that he has nothing to say at this time and that he has no personal concerns right now. The Third Account and Report of Trustee, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Aug-14-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Caroline Hinshaw for and with Dongna Fang.
David Huang (Beneficiary and Settlor of the Trust) also present.

Ms. Hinshaw explained that David's Dad died in 1998 and that his Mom died in 2015 leaving him as the sole beneficiary and trustee of the parent's trust, that this trust was established out of the conservatorship, and that what had to collapse was the parents' survivor's trust, the Marital trust and the exemption trust and put into this trust. Ms. Hinshaw also stated that there has three trustees collecting rents, that it was a mess, that they are now totally coordinated and that there are 20 pages of disbursements. The Court made reference to the order requiring time sheets. Ms. Fang stated that she has been keeping time sheets for this year. The Court informed the Parties that it wants to see the time sheets for the next accounting. Ms. Hinshaw stated that they are thinking of selling or exchanging the Richmond property, that the supplement she submitted has a list of the disbursements by date, that the unlawful detainer Attorney (Mr. Chen) is having a difficult time with the eviction of Mr. Jones, that there is another issue with the Sister of one of the tenants living in a property who is not paying rent, that Mr. Chen is looking for a cause of action to deal with the issue because she does not fall under tenants law because she does not pay rent and that Mr. Chen has only been paid $5,000.00 out of the $25,000.00 that was approved. Ms. Fang stated that David does get upset with Mr. Jones. The Amended Second Account and Report of Trustee, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jan-25-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Caroline Hinshaw, for and with Petitioner Dongna Fang and also with David Huang.

Ms. Hinshaw stated that she filed a supplemental declaration on Monday, December 24, which had the exhibits, including bank statements, not previously filed. She stated that all rental payments were accounted for except a single payment of $1821, which it was believed had been sent to the prior trustee Yu Tong Quong by a tenant who initially refused to recognize Ms. Fang as the substituted trustee. She reported that Ms. Fang spent much of her time caring for the needs of David Huang. She said that since he does not cook, she has arranged for prepared food to be delivered to him. She reported that she was no longer using the law firm of Sheppard Uziel, but pursauant to court order had obtained authorization to engage the legal services of Jeff Chen to remove a defaulting tenant. She stated that Mr. Huang does not have direct access to trust assets, but receives a monthly allowance. Mr. Huang acknowledged that the allowance was adequate for his needs. The Court directed that the trustee prepare and submit timesheets going forwward in future accountings. Ms. Hinshaw stated that she has the information to prepare the next accounting, and should have it on file within the next couple of weeks. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Anasse. (Not reported)
